name: Divisibility and Perfect Squares
description: Analyzing properties of perfect squares in relation to divisibility rules (e.g., multiples of 24 means multiples of 3 and 8).

name: Kinematics (Relative Motion & Time/Distance)
description: Solving problems involving distances, speeds, and times, including relative motion scenarios and effects of breaks.

name: Complex Number Arithmetic
description: Performing operations (powers, multiplication) with complex numbers and extracting real/imaginary parts.

name: Least Common Multiple (LCM)
description: Finding the least common multiple to determine when periodic events align.

name: Function Iteration and Fixed Points
description: Analyzing the behavior of a function when applied repeatedly and finding values that remain unchanged after transformations.

name: Recurrence Relations
description: Solving problems involving sequences defined by recurrence relations, including dynamic programming for counting paths/sequences.

name: Floor/Ceiling Functions and Modular Arithmetic
description: Applying properties of floor and ceiling functions in conjunction with modular arithmetic.

name: Polynomial Roots and Properties
description: Using Vieta's formulas, factor theorem, and other polynomial properties to analyze roots (integer, complex, real) and coefficients, including in probabilistic contexts.

name: Inscribed and Tangent Circles
description: Solving geometric problems involving properties of inscribed circles, tangent lines, and their relationships with triangles and other polygons.

name: Constrained Arrangements
description: Counting arrangements of objects on grids or in sequences with various specific constraints (e.g., row/column sums, run-lengths, colors, rotational symmetry).

name: Number Base Conversion and Digit Properties
description: Working with different number bases, analyzing properties related to the sum of digits, and counting numbers with specific digit characteristics (e.g., geometric sequences of digits, parity-monotonic).

name: Coordinate Geometry and Transformations
description: Applying coordinate geometry principles and geometric transformations (e.g., rotation, linear transformations) to calculate areas, distances, and new coordinates.

name: 3D Geometry (Plane Intersections & Folding)
description: Analyzing three-dimensional geometric figures, calculating areas of cross-sections formed by plane intersections, and properties of solids formed by folding 2D shapes.

name: Advanced Triangle Geometry
description: Applying complex geometric theorems related to circumcircles, incenters, centroids, angle bisectors, and tangent lines in triangles, including area calculations.

name: Probability Theory (Combinatorial & Geometric)
description: Calculating probabilities for discrete and continuous events, including binomial probability, conditional probability, geometric probability, and counting outcomes under various constraints.

name: Factors and Sums
description: Analyzing properties of factors of integers and their sums in ordered triples.

name: Vector and Coordinate Geometry
description: Using vectors or coordinates to solve geometric problems involving lengths, distances, and areas.

name: Systems of Linear Equations (Work Rate)
description: Solving problems involving work rates and production using systems of linear equations.

name: Lattice Point Counting
description: Counting integer coordinate points within a specified geometric region defined by inequalities.

name: Inequalities with Floor Function
description: Solving inequalities involving the floor function.

name: Circle Geometry and Optimization
description: Applying properties of circles and perpendicular lines to solve optimization problems involving lengths.

name: Summation and Modular Arithmetic
description: Calculating sums of series and finding remainders using modular arithmetic.

name: Volume Optimization
description: Optimizing the volume of a 3D object under given constraints.

name: Sequences and Powers
description: Analyzing properties of sequences involving powers of integers.

name: Vieta's Formulas (Cubic)
description: Using Vieta's formulas to relate the roots and coefficients of a cubic polynomial.

name: Trigonometric Identities
description: Applying trigonometric identities to simplify expressions and solve problems.

name: Complex Numbers (Geometric Transformations)
description: Using complex numbers to represent and perform geometric transformations like rotation and translation.

name: Non-Linear Systems and Optimization
description: Solving systems of non-linear algebraic equations and finding maximum/minimum values of expressions.

name: Properties of Cubes and Squares
description: Analyzing integers that can be expressed as differences of cubes and simultaneously as perfect squares.

name: Parallelogram Properties
description: Applying geometric properties of parallelograms and vector/coordinate geometry to find lengths.

name: Subset Counting
description: Counting subsets of integers with various constraints (e.g., adjacency, specific sums, or properties).

name: Systems of Diophantine Equations
description: Solving systems of equations for positive integer solutions.

name: Trigonometry and Coordinate Geometry
description: Applying trigonometric principles and coordinate geometry to solve problems involving distances and angles.

name: Logarithmic Functions
description: Solving equations and analyzing properties involving logarithms and trigonometric functions.

name: Integer Representations
description: Counting the number of ways an integer can be represented under specific conditions.

name: Determinants and Number Theory
description: Calculating determinants of matrices and applying number theory concepts like relative primality.

name: Area Calculation (Various Shapes)
description: Calculating areas of various geometric shapes, including polygons, regions defined by functions (e.g., floor function), and combinations of shapes.

name: Regular Polygons
description: Analyzing properties of regular polygons, including midpoints, area ratios, and relationships with rays.

name: Mixture Problems
description: Solving problems involving mixing solutions with different concentrations.

name: Polygon Angles and Arithmetic Sequences
description: Applying properties of polygon interior angles and arithmetic sequences to find angle measures.

name: Angle Bisectors
description: Applying properties of angle bisectors in triangles, including their intersections with perpendiculars and midpoints.

name: Ratios and Proportions
description: Solving problems involving proportional relationships and quantities.

name: Arithmetic Sequences
description: Analyzing properties of arithmetic sequences and finding minimum/maximum values.

name: Exponential Equations
description: Solving equations involving exponents and rational numbers.

name: Divisibility Properties
description: Analyzing divisibility rules and properties of products of integers.

name: Modular Arithmetic (Powers)
description: Calculating remainders of powers and sums of elements using modular arithmetic.
